mnemonics.title: Types of Pollution
mnemonics.content: A W S N R T - Air, Water, Soil, Noise, Radioactive, Thermal

mnemonics.title: Air Pollutants
mnemonics.content: S O N P C - Sulfur oxides, Oxides of nitrogen, Particulate matter, Carbon monoxide, Hydrocarbons

mnemonics.title: Pollution Effects on Health
mnemonics.content: R C C - Respiratory problems, Cancer, Cardiovascular issues

mnemonics.title: Control Measures
mnemonics.content: C L E A N - Clean energy, Laws/regulations, Effluent treatment, Awareness, New technology

acronyms.usage: Measures air pollution levels; AQI > 100 is unhealthy
Indicates water pollution level; higher BOD = more pollution
Indian regulatory body for pollution control
Ozone-depleting substances used in refrigerators and aerosols
Tiny particles in air; PM2.5 and PM10 are key pollutants
Discarded electronic devices containing toxic materials

visualTips.visualization: Picture a pie chart divided into 6 equal slices: Air (30%), Water (25%), Soil (20%), Noise (10%), Radioactive (8%), Thermal (7%) - each representing percentage of pollution contribution
Imagine a food chain pyramid: phytoplankton (low toxin) → small fish (medium) → large fish (high) → humans (highest toxin concentration)

visualTips.visualize: Factory smoke + rain cloud = acidic rain falling on buildings, trees, and lakes causing damage
Green algae covering a lake like a blanket, blocking sunlight and oxygen for fish below

analogies.analogy: Like a dirty filter in a car engine - when the air filter gets clogged, the engine doesn't work properly; similarly, when air gets polluted, our lungs cannot function properly
Like adding too much dye to a clear glass of water - once contaminated, it's difficult to restore to original state
Like stacking blocks - each level adds more blocks on top, making the top level carry the accumulated weight of all blocks below
Like sunscreen for Earth - just as sunscreen protects our skin from harmful UV rays, the ozone layer protects the entire planet
Like measuring how many hungry guests will eat at a party - more guests (microorganisms) need more food (oxygen) to eat the waste (organic matter)
